rationale: In the theory section, the citing paper uses Janakiraman, Sismeiro, and Dutta (2009) to support a core mechanism: perceived similarity increases accessibility and diagnosticity, which in turn enables spillover across competing nameplates. This is not just a background list citation; it is used to justify why perverse halo should occur and to motivate boundary-condition reasoning about when spillovers are stronger. Although the citing paper does not reuse the 2009 paper's structural model, it relies on that prior spillover logic in its conceptual setup.
relation_description: The citing paper draws on Janakiraman et al. (2009) as conceptual grounding for competitive spillovers driven by similarity, then tests that logic in a new context of social-media chatter around product recalls. The cited work functions as prior theoretical and empirical support for the spillover mechanism used in hypothesis development.
